title: Personality Disorders
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Personality Disorders
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Personality Disorders
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Personality Disorders** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Personality Disorders. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Personality Disorders, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Personality Disorders.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Personality Disorders
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Personality Disorders?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Personality Disorders, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Personality Disorders?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Personality Disorders, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Personality Disorders requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Personality Disorders requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.
